author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo Switch Sports Resort confirms 100 Pin Bowling, here’s a first look

Nintendo Switch Sports Resort, which was recent announced, has confirmed that 100 Pin Bowling will be included. Nintendo itself shared the news, posting a screenshot on its Japanese website. 100 Pin Bowling was a part of the original Wii Sports Resort. It’s still ultimately about bowling, but the mode is set up in a triangular formation and obviously has you...

The post Nintendo Switch Sports Resort confirms 100 Pin Bowling, here’s a first look appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

text:
New evidence emerges of Diablo 4 coming to Nintendo Switch 2

Will we eventually see Diablo 4 on Nintendo Switch 2? Additional evidence has to come light that points in that direction. A Nintendo Switch 2 version of the game has been rated in Taiwan – specifically for Lord of Hatred, one of the game’s expansions. Technically it happened at the very end of April – but it either went unnoticed,...

text:
List of all prices for Nintendo Switch 2 Upgrade Packs

Various store pages confirm pricing for all Nintendo Switch 2 Upgrade Packs that will be available. There are a variety of price points, with some costing as much as $20. On the other hand, many are free – those these are from third-parties. Here’s the full roundup: 4PGP – $4.99 A-Train: All Aboard Tourism – $6.99 Animal Crossing: New Horizons...

text:
List of all Nintendo Switch 2 games that support GameShare

With GameShare becoming an important part of Nintendo Switch 2, we’ve put together a complete list of games that support the feature. We’ll be updating this post over time as well. GameShare is brand new for Nintendo Switch 2. With the feature, select titles allow you to play with others even if they don’t own a copy of the game....

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Kami 2 on the way to Nintendo Switch, more than 10 years after the original on 3DS

Kami 2 is coming to Nintendo Switch very shortly, according to a listing on the eShop. We’ll be getting it on July 15, 2026. If the name Kami sounds familiar, that’s because we saw the original title on Nintendo 3DS. The relaxing puzzle game is now getting a sequel on Nintendo Switch more than a decade later with over 100...

text:
Nintendo Switch eShop charts – June 20, 2026 – top 10 is looking very familiar

The latest Nintendo Switch eShop charts for the week of June 20, 2026 are now available. Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream, as usual, is leading here at #1. text:
#Drive Rally launch trailer – arcade rally racing game debuts on Nintendo Switch

Following this week’s launch, PM Studios and Pixel Perfect Dude sent out a launch trailer for #Drive Rally. The arcade rally racing game came to Nintendo Switch this week. A bunch of information about it can be found in the following overview: Combining accessible pick-up-and-play controls with rewarding depth, #Drive Rally challenges players to master a variety of stages across...

text:
A complete history of Star Fox 64 retellings and differences between each one

We previously wrote about five things we want to see from a new Star Fox game. In case you missed our list, here’s a summarized version: accessible controls, fantastic graphics, an Arwing amiibo, a new story, and a villain that isn’t Andross. Unfortunately, we correctly predicted that a new Star Fox game would in fact feature Andross as the main...

text:
You can now save $10 on physical pre-orders for Nintendo Switch Sports Resort, Fire Emblem, Orbitals, more

A few upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 games that you can pre-order are currently on sale, with Nintendo Switch Sports Resort, Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave, and Orbitals now $10 off. text:

NIS America published a launch trailer for R-Type Tactics 1-2 Cosmos. The package, which was announced in 2022, finally launched in the west this week after multiple delays.
